title: Create profiles table SQL migration
description: Write and run the SQL migration that creates the profiles table in Supabase. This table is the single source of truth for all user metadata beyond what Supabase Auth stores natively.
acceptance_criteria: Table `profiles` exists in the public schema
Column `id` is UUID, primary key, references auth.users(id) with ON DELETE CASCADE
Column `role` is text with CHECK constraint allowing only 'tenant' or 'landlord' or NULL
Column `wallet_address` is text, nullable, with a UNIQUE constraint
Column `onboarding_complete` is boolean, default false, not null
Column `created_at` is timestamptz, default now(), not null

steps.details: Navigate to your Supabase project dashboard → SQL Editor → New query.

steps.description: Write and run the CREATE TABLE statement
steps.details:
CREATE TABLE public.profiles (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  role TEXT CHECK (role IN ('tenant', 'landlord')),
  display_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  avatar_url TEXT,
  wallet_address TEXT UNIQUE,
  onboarding_complete BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
Run: SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'profiles'; — confirm all 7 columns are present.

technical_notes: ON DELETE CASCADE ensures that if a user is deleted from auth.users, their profile row is automatically removed. wallet_address uses EIP-55 checksummed format — enforce this in application code before insertion.
